game.title: Lifeline
game.short_text: VimJam - There and back

details.title: Lifeline
details.description:
Welcome to Lifeline

Made for VimJam #1

_____
In the game you must survive by collecting lifenodes while being chased by enemies that appear from the nodes you created! I made the game during 2 evenings, I made my best to do the game as fast as possible so I made it very minimalistic to be able to do within 10h max (which I challenged myself to), it was still way easier by having time to think between each coding session.


_____
I gained time by using sound designs made by Romain Rope for a previous game jam and musics from Scott Buckley :- Sleep (intro music)- Tears in Rain (game music)
_____
Game can be played with mouse only but it is not very well optimized and it uses a LOT of objects to create trails so it might lags in late game!Even it is a small game I hope you will enjoy and thanks for playing!

game.title: THERE AND BACK - THE SHOW
game.short_text: VimJam Submission (#11 Overall - #1 Theme/Limitation)

details.title: THERE AND BACK - THE SHOW
details.description:
CONTROLS (MOUSE REQUIRED):WASD  = moveUse mouse to face left, right, up, or downLeft Mouse = attackRight Mouse = dashTab = base finderNotice:Some players have missed that you can upgrade your alien in the upgrade menu. There are arrows in the upgrade menu that lets you change to upgrading your alien and back to upgrading your human.
DESCRIPTION:BINGE WATCH THE ALL NEW SEASON OF "THERE AND BACK!" -- A gameshow where contestants forage a savage planet for the opportunity to return homeEach day both contestants must venture out "THERE" to collect gems and then return "BACK" to base before running out of oxygen.Why do the contestants need these gems? To upgrade and train their super killer alien battle pet of course! I mean, they could use their gems on themselves, but that's not going to help them in the epic 1v1 alien vs alien winner takes all show down.Yes that's right! After 5 days of collecting gems, both contestants aliens will fight each other. The winner get's to go back to Earth. The loser gets eaten by BlobbyBlob TentacleThing!This game was made in 1 week for the VimJam!

game.title: Off-Brand Hero
game.short_text: Vimjam Game Jam entry - 9th place

details.title: Off-Brand Hero
details.description:
SUBMISSION FOR VIMJAM 2020

Ranked out of  465 entries
Play as a cute kid transported to a world of imagination while trying to escape from her greatest enemy! While there, she will COLLECT the powers of various copyright friendly costumes and build confidence in herself until she can ultimately return to her home, confront the enemy, and make it THERE AND BACK AGAIN. 
The game is playable to completion between 5-15 minutes and it's not terribly hard. The ending is my favorite part so I hope you get to see it! I will include a dev log with hints if you need help progressing!
If you're looking for secrets (i.e. hidden rooms), try investigating the main villain a little more :)
All artwork, music, code and sound effects were created specifically for this jam. My main focus for this jam was POLISH. I wanted to make a condensed version of a game that you might see published. Overall, I'm incredibly proud of what I've put together. 
Controller support is enabled as well, though it was a last minute addition so it doesn't say in the game. On an xbox controller use the following: A - Jump, B - Change costume, X - Special move, Analog stick moves.
Check out my devlog if you want to see how I made it!


Download, extract and play :)

game.short_text: Rescue your daughter from the interdimensional dangers

details.title: Rewinder
details.description:
What will you do to bring your daughter back to safety?
Rewinder is a short twin-stick shooter, made for VimJam: Collectables (8 Bits to Infinity). Shoot your way across this strange dimension and rewind your mistakes by saving your daughter's life.
You can play with Keyboard & Mouse (left-handed config. included) or Controller (Xbox One/360 recommended).
Code and Art by @ricenoodlesSFX:  Kenney & Juhani JunkalaMusic:  "Stand With Us", "Constellation" and "The Void" by Trevor LentzFont: Pixellari by Zacchary Dempsey-Plante
*-*-*-*-*-*-*-*-*SPOILERS AHEAD*-*-*-*-*-*-*-*-*

-How to obtain the 4th node: https://gyazo.com/23de3b7ef52b5a36d5375c058dc6ff3a
-To unlock the secret ending, you will need a total of 16 nodes. To achieve that, craft the first 15 and head north from your base. At one point, you will see a lonely plant, head WEST from there and you will find the last and secret cluster.  Backtrack to craft your new and last node, and head back north again. Once you reach the final zone, don't activate the capsule and head EAST, you will find there the secret ending.

Crash landed on an alien planet without any weapons to defend yourself, you find a support drone that can help you on your way. Can you use it find new ship parts and get off this planet?

game.title: Hop Hop Miner
game.short_text: Hop Hop Miner is an action-procedural-permadeath game where you need to collect as many golden gem as possible!

details.title: Hop Hop Miner
details.description:
Hop Hop Miner is an action-procedural game where you need to collect as many golden gem as possible. All the caves are made procedurally so they are different every time (so if you loose, try again!). You start only with 3 lives and there is a permadeath, so choose wisely upgrades from the shop!How many golden gems can you collect? Post the screenshot down below! :)
This games was made in 7 days for the VimJam with jam focus on "collectables" and a theme "there and back".For collectables I decided to go for the gems that are crucial in this game since you need them in order to buy upgrades and proceed.For the theme "there and back"  the player need to collect the golden gem and carry it back. 
This is my first time with procedural generation, so be understanding! XDFollow me on twitter if you like my game @hav24  
Controls:   
- Arrow keys or WASD to move (you can move only left and right).- Mouse click to buy upgrades from the shop.
Credits:       
All assets were made by me. Alessandro Pezzetti aka havana24 (except for the music, credit below).8bit sound effects made with Bfxr.
Background music by Playonloop.com  Pixel font by Kenney.nl.
A game made by havana24.net Twitter @hav24

resultData.title: Teddy Bear Story - A Fabian Velander Creation

game.title: Teddy Bear Story - A Fabian Velander Creation
game.short_text: Cope with your daughters death through experimental drugs.

details.title: Teddy Bear Story - A Fabian Velander Creation
details.description:
"Teddy Bear Story is a heartbreaking game where you're trying to fulfill your daughters last wish. I've never cared more for a capsule than I did while playing this game." - Vimlark


"It really is an artistic achievement."  - H.H. Magoo
"WOW such a good game, the best story in this jam!" - amitklein
"A tragic little vignette with very polished production values." - DrManticore

Read the many more reviews on this games submission page :)







Coping with loss can be hard.
Your daughter died in a car crash.
There was nothing you could do to save her, and you know that.
The car crash wasn't your fault, and you know that.
Looking for a teddy bear should not be a priority when your child is bleeding out on the ground, and you know that.
But you still can't forgive yourself for ignoring her last wish.
Made for VimJam: Collectables (8 Bits to Infinity)Download --> Unzip --> Play

moreInfo.Author: fabian velander
moreInfo.Tags: 3D, Abstract, artgame, Atmospheric, Dark, First-Person, Narrative, Singleplayer, Story Rich, Unity

game.title: Legato
game.short_text: Follow the musical journey of a young boy, who someday hopes to be a composer.

details.title: Legato
details.description:
Note:  I recommend using keyboard for this game, as controller tool tips aren't present. The puzzles in the game also require number buttons 1 through 5.
I also recommend playing with the downloadable version, as the web browser version is a tad bit buggy in terms of audio and visuals, which is a big problem for this game. However, it does seem playable for those who can't download the game.

Music is such an important part of our lives. It has the ability to move others, and let them experience emotions, simply through sound. This young boy has been obsessed with music and playing the piano, and wants to make a difference in the world through his joy of piano. In this game, you play as the young boy, and follow him in the emotional imagination that is his own music and song. You take an entire journey to his dream, and then safely return back to the comfort of his own home.
Also, the theme of this game was not taken super literally. Keep in mind when you finish a puzzle, you can continue down the path. You don't have to go back from where you came.
Controls are explained in the game for the most part, but for those who want to use a combination of controller for movement and keyboard for the actual piano, here are those:
Movement:  A and S  OR  Left Stick Left and Right
Jump:  W / Space Bar  OR Controller A
Crystal Listen/Music Staff (White) Door Open:   E  OR  Controller X
PIANO: (number keys)  | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 |

I also really just want to give both Vimlark and the 8-Bits to Infinity Community a big thank you for such an amazing jam. I'm stoked to see what everybody else made, and I'm hoping you all will enjoy my game as well!

Twitter: https://twitter.com/SGruntled
Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCIzce-d35l1aVbZpDM8jNYQ
**If you are experiencing trouble with the browser version of the game, a .exe file of the game is also accessible through a download.**

game.title: I Command Thee
game.short_text: You take the role of a manager with the mantra: avoid working at all costs !

details.title: I Command Thee
details.description:
Controls
Receive a mail -> Give to employee -> Stamp -> Send -> Done
Mouse Drag & Drop. Right click on a mail to see its content.
Avoid getting too many mails waiting around, or you'll get overwhelmed with them !
__
Description
I Command Thee is a satirical game, in which you take the role of a manager, that has one single purpose : avoid working at all costs !
To achieve this, you'll have to make sure you delegate tasks to your slaves interns, stamp them and quickly send them back to your clients. You also must get rid of all those pesky junk mails to avoid getting overwhelmed by them !
__
This game was made for the 2020 VimJam Game Jam, and had to comply with two themes :
There and back : This theme is taken quite literally by the emails, that have to go there and back, between you, the ones to actually do the tasks, you again, and the clients !
Collectables : Is this game, you collect emails, which you absolutely do not want ! If you collect too many of them, this means game over. Experience being afraid of watching that collectable meter going higher and higher, completely out of your control !

moreInfo.Authors: Anaba-Games, Nathan, Antoine Brousse, Matías Godoy - Zoundbie, Rowgue
moreInfo.Tags: 2D, Arcade, Difficult, Game Jam, Management, Short, Singleplayer

details.title: Corrupted Dread - VimJam
details.description:
READ DESCRIPTION - VimJam2020
A critical error causes you to be accidentally teleported in the middle of the corruption zone, fight your way out and make it back to the lab.
Please read the credits in game. I also included what I learned from the jam :)

How it related to the jam:
Samples are collectables, that's easy to explain. I have a feeling the "there and back" might be a little harder to understand. Originally, the ending was involving making it back to the lab, but due to time restraints I wasn't able to complete the ending fully. So instead I added a weapon upgrade system, but there's only one per level, requiring the player to go "there (to the collectables) and back (to upgrade their weapon)". The reason I point this out is because I'm worried that it might go over peoples head, and people might try and call me out for not following the theme. I hope you understand, and have fun playing my short little game :)

game.title: Ghostel
game.short_text: A short game for the Vimlark + 8Bits to Infinity game jam (VimJam)

details.title: Ghostel
details.description:
EDIT: Updated to support gamepad controls, fix the bug with input lock relating to signpost in secret room, and tweak camera follow settings a little bit.---------------------------------------------------------------------You find yourself trapped inside the Ghostel Inn. What is this place? How did you get here? What's with these purple walls, when was this place last decorated?
The front door is locked! You must find a way to open the door and reach your freedom. On the way you'll be collecting these strange orbs of incorporeal matter. 
You wonder what manner of beings left these spirit orbs here, but before you leave, you'll have to travel to the other side and back.
---------------------------------------------------------------------
Ghostel (Ghost-Hotel) Is a working title for this small platform/puzzle game for the VimJam. The theme was "There and Back" with a requirement for the game to contain some form of collectables. So in Ghostel you're goal is to collect as many spirit orbs as possible whilst trying to find your way out of this unusual place. 
---------------------------------------------------------------------
Random Dev Thoughts / Post Mortem:
Originally the collectables were going to have more mechanical value than just a form of 'score'.  If the project see's any more life the pickups would almost certainly become some form of usable currency, be that for items, unlocks, or perhaps replacing the machines as a way to enter your incorporeal form whilst draining your spirit reserves... I guess we'll see what the future may hold for the project if I revisit it.
This is the first time I've tried to create a large single world style level for a game, so trying to layout rooms and routes that felt open whilst still ensuring players would be able to be where they needed to be was an interesting challenge. It is also a huge step forward in terms of my practicing pixel art and it was great fun trying to create assets that (for the most part) felt like they followed a consistent style.
I think if I was going to use Unity TileMaps to build levels in the future I'd likely want some more advanced custom tools to help with room creation, as swapping between multiple tilemap layers each time you want to build a room was tiresome.Extract the project from the downloaded zip folder and just run the Ghostel.exe application.

game.title: Alchemist Blast
game.short_text: Casual high score jump game for the vim game jam

details.title: Alchemist Blast
details.description:
Casual high score game made for the VIMJAM  (8 Bits to Infinity), made in solo by myself, Alariann (Alariann Games).  It's been started around 3 days before the deadline.
The goal is to go as high as possible!
Click on the upper part of the screen (when the tutorial is finished and the potion shop is closed) to jump in that direction!

For that, you can use potions that you throw while in midair (first click pauses the game so you have time to choose where you want the potion to explode).
The blue potions explosion give you a push in the desired direction. Click below you to push you upward, click on the side to be pushed to the other side etc...
 (blue potions give you a push, but later other potions will be added to the game with other cool effects!)

game.title: Hurrah, Hurrah!
game.short_text: Frantic antics by frantic ants

details.title: Hurrah, Hurrah!
details.description:
Direct a colony of ants to collect as many crumbs as possible before the music stops. 
'Hurrah Hurrah' is a family-friendly arcade game with multiple strategies.
Who made this game? 
H. H. Magoo did all of the game design, programming, sound effects, photography, and art. A trio of her friends who wish to be identified only as 'Queenie and the Drones' sang 'The Ants Go Marching'. 
How does it meet the requirements of 8 Bits to Infinity's VimJam? 
The goal of the game is to collect crumbs. Your ants must safely bring the crumbs back to the nest before they are added to your score. As for optional challenges, Magoo was inspired by the theme of 8 Bits to Infinity's arcade jam, "swarm", and each round of the game lasts for the length of the song 'The Ants Go Marching'.
Why is this game fun?
There is an interesting "it takes money to make money" tension regarding which of the six Enh-ants-ments to purchase and in which order. They all measurably improve performance but not always enough to cover their cost.
Any strategy suggestions? 
For your first game, I recommend purchasing Olfactovision as soon as you have 5 crumbs, and then Jumbo Ants.

game.title: Expecting Groceries
game.short_text: Shopping isn't for the light hearted.

details.title: Expecting Groceries
details.description:
Getting groceries gets wild for soon-to-be dad Lester Quest.
One moment, he’s checking out; the next his beautiful and -very- pregnant wife, Madge Quest, is overcome with ravenous cravings.
  
He knows there isn’t much time before the check-out clerk loses his cool, but Lester insists on making his wife happy–at any cost.
  
This punny, super dad knows the clock is ticking.  Summoning his sacred “Dad Powers” he’ll dodge around grocery hazards to get whatever his beloved desires.   
However, when the sample lady throws deadly shrimp, Lester has to decide what is more important: getting the goods, or staying alive?
Controls:
Movement Keys: W, A, S, D
[Right-click] to channel your Dad Powers into a concentrated burst of speed towards the mouse. 
[SPACEBAR] to discuss food strategies with your pregnant wife. 
Keep your stress down by dodging past rolling cans, flying jams, and scampi bullets. 
Navigate the treacherous grocery to retrieve the ingredients for a dish most magnificent before time's up! 
**This game was created for VimJam 2020 with a focus on collectables and a theme of "There and Back".  This is our first game and our first jam!  Hope you enjoy.**
Credits:
Code and Concept by Woolamander (Goats Gruff Games)Art and Animation by Annuke (Goats Gruff Games)
Cover Font by FenotypeBase Sprite Art by Lokif (Changes made: some poses, hair, eyes, clothes, and shoes)
Music:Gameplay Theme "Sweet 70s" composed by Clement PanchoutMainMenu Theme "CVP701Jam" composed by omfgdudeGameOver Theme "The Charm 68" composed by The Cynic Project - https://pixelsphere.org/
Various sound effects composed by:Wobbleboxx.comartisticdudequbodup
Download zip file.Extract folders from zip file.Click on "expecting groceries" application with the headshot icon.Enjoy!
